whyTokenIsBad: GitHub returned HTTP 401 Bad credentials for the provided ghp_ token (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).
A 401 from /user means the token is not accepted at all — it has been revoked, expired, or was never valid.
Classic GitHub PATs (ghp_...) can be revoked by the owner, deleted, or expire automatically depending on their settings.
The Rork-managed ghs_ clone token embedded in the github remote is also rejected now ('Invalid username or token'), so the direct GitHub push path is currently blocked.
The only working push path is the Rork git router (origin remote), which updated to b42e44c but did not mirror to the public GitHub repo in this turn.
To restore direct GitHub API/push access, generate a new fine-grained or classic PAT with 'repo' scope at https://github.com/settings/tokens and paste it here. It will be saved and tested.
githubTokenExplanation.savedTo: GITHUB_TOKEN is saved in the local expo/.env file (gitignored). Production IVX owner-variables store requires a working owner-auth session; the stored owner password/token in this sandbox did not authenticate against the live backend.
